goal: MVP install + setup hardening: fix go.mod module path so 'go install github.com/mainline-org/mainline@latest' works, add 'mainline init --rewire' to fix refspec on existing repos with origin added later, extend 'mainline doctor' with a --setup mode that verifies remote / refspec / identity / agents.md and suggests fixes

summary.title: MVP install + setup hardening: go.mod path, init --rewire, doctor --setup
summary.what: Three coordinated MVP-readiness fixes for handing the project to a friend who will use it on their own repo with a collaborator. (1) go.mod module path migrated from bare 'mainline' to github.com/mainline-org/mainline so that `go install github.com/mainline-org/mainline@latest` actually works; 50 import sites rewritten via sed, build + tests pass. (2) Init's refspec configuration extracted into Service.configureRemoteRefspecs — idempotent, no-op without origin, single source of truth. New Service.Rewire and `mainline init --rewire` re-apply refspec config + AGENTS.md + PR template without touching identity or team config; init's success message now prints a friendly hint when origin is missing telling the user to come back with --rewire after adding one. (3) Service.doctorSetup and `mainline doctor --setup` produce a structured DoctorSetupReport with ten boolean checks (HasOrigin, NotesFetchOK, NotesPushOK, ActorFetchOK, ActorPushOK, NotesDisplayRefOK, IdentityOK, AgentsMDOK, PRTemplateOK, GitignoreOK) plus Issues and Fixed slices; --fix wires missing refspecs in place. README install + Daily commands table updated.
summary.why: Pre-MVP review surfaced three first-day blockers for a new user: (a) the README's `go install mainline@latest` line could not work because go.mod's bare 'mainline' module name is not a fully-qualified module path; new users would just get '404'; (b) running `mainline init` before adding `git remote add origin ...` silently skipped notes / actor-log refspec configuration with no warning, no recovery path, and no way to detect the misconfiguration — sync would just appear to be a single-actor repo; (c) when sync misbehaves there was no diagnostic command — `mainline doctor` only knew how to find orphan local drafts. With a friend about to use this for real collaboration, all three bugs would show up in their first week.
summary.user_goal: Hand the project to a friend confident that `go install` works, `mainline init` is recoverable, and `mainline doctor --setup` is a one-stop diagnostic when anything looks off.

decisions.rationale: That's where go install will actually fetch from; matches existing remote URL; mirrors community convention. Any other path would require extra Go module proxy / vanity URL setup we do not want to operate.
rejected: Keep bare 'mainline' and document `git clone && go build` as the only install path — works but rules out the standard Go install flow forever; bad for adoption.
Use a vanity URL like mainline.dev/cli — overhead without payoff at this scale.

decisions.chose: Service.configureRemoteRefspecs (engine package, exported as a Service method)
decisions.rationale: Three callers need it (Init, Rewire, doctor --setup --fix). Putting it on Service keeps state access (Git, identity) in scope without re-plumbing. Returning the slice of additions makes the doctor report and rewire output trivially honest about what changed.

decisions.point: Rewire vs a fresh Init re-run
decisions.chose: Separate Rewire flag/method that explicitly does not touch identity or team config
decisions.rationale: Re-running Init on an initialised repo errors out by design ('already initialized'). Loosening that rule risks clobbering identity. A dedicated rewire path is more honest about what it does, and the CLI flag (`--rewire`) makes the diff between 'set me up' and 'fix my wiring' obvious.

decisions.chose: Boolean per check + Issues []string for messages + Fixed []string for what --fix wrote
decisions.rationale: Ten booleans drive the ✓/✗ rendering and let JSON consumers ask 'is X ok' programmatically. Issues are the same strings the human render prints — keeping them in the JSON means agents can read and act on them without reparsing the bool fields. Fixed is empty unless --fix actually changed something.

decisions.point: Init's friendly hint about missing origin
decisions.chose: Print a 3-line note after init when origin is absent
decisions.rationale: A silent skip used to leave users wondering why their cross-actor sync did nothing. Inline message at the moment of init avoids that even for users who do not later run doctor.

risks: go.mod path change is a coordinated import rewrite across 50 files — easy to miss one. Build + full quick-test verifies, but anything not in the test set could still hit a stale import (skim of grep output suggests there is none).

Rewire deletes-then-rewrites AGENTS.md and the PR template; if a user has hand-edited AGENTS.md their changes are lost. Documented in the help text but worth a louder warning if dogfood shows users do edit it.

Doctor --setup's gitignore check is a substring match for `.ml-cache/` — won't catch an .ml-cache entry that lacks the trailing slash. False-negative is the safer direction (doctor stays quiet) but worth tightening if it ever bites.
followups: PR B (collab UX): seal-submit prints a 'next: mainline pr-description' hint; mainline check <intent> shortcut for prepare; mainline init --actor-id <id> for cross-machine identity reuse; README FAQ entry for non-Claude-Code IDEs (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot).
Sync should call configureRemoteRefspecs on first run if origin exists but refspecs are missing — saves a manual rewire step.
Tighten doctor's gitignore substring check to a real line-prefix match.
Consider whether Rewire should leave hand-edited AGENTS.md alone (compare hash with the template before overwriting).

architectural_claims: go.mod module path matches the repo URL — `go install github.com/mainline-org/mainline@latest` works.
Refspec configuration is idempotent and has a single implementation; three callers (Init, Rewire, doctor) share it.
doctor --setup is the canonical 'is this install healthy' command.
behavioral_changes: go install github.com/mainline-org/mainline@latest now produces a working binary.
mainline init prints a friendly hint when origin is not configured.
mainline init --rewire re-applies refspec config + AGENTS.md + PR template without re-creating identity.
mainline doctor --setup runs install / wiring sanity checks; --fix wires missing refspecs.

api_changes.signature: engine.DoctorOptions gains Setup bool; DoctorResult gains Setup *DoctorSetupReport

migration_notes: External Go consumers importing 'mainline/internal/...' must change to 'github.com/mainline-org/mainline/internal/...'. Internal-only paths so the blast radius is small.
End-users running `make build` keep working; only `go install` callers need the new module path.
